collections.name: Daily Inspiration
collections.description: Start your day with motivational quotes from visionary leaders and thinkers.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
You miss 100% of the shots you don't take.

collections.name: Grit & Perseverance
collections.description: Words for pushing through hardship, failing forward, and refusing to quit.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Ever tried. Ever failed. No matter. Try again. Fail again. Fail better.
Grit is passion and perseverance for very long-term goals.

collections.name: Leadership & Vision
collections.description: On leading people, casting vision, and rising to the moment.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Plans are worthless, but planning is everything.
The price of greatness is responsibility.

collections.name: Courage & Conviction
collections.description: On facing fear, standing for what's right, and acting anyway.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Courage is resistance to fear, mastery of fear — not absence of fear.
Power concedes nothing without a demand. It never did and it never will.

collections.name: Dream Big
collections.description: On ambition, chasing goals, and daring to imagine what's possible.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Stay hungry. Stay foolish.
Hitch your wagon to a star.

collections.name: Stoic Wisdom
collections.description: Timeless philosophy from Marcus Aurelius, Seneca, and Epictetus to guide your life.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: You have power over your mind - not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.

collections.name: Creative Minds
collections.description: Insights on creativity and innovation from artists, writers, and inventors.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Imagination is more important than knowledge. Knowledge is limited. Imagination encircles the world.
Every child is an artist. The problem is how to remain an artist once we grow up.

collections.name: Tech Visionaries
collections.description: Words of wisdom from the pioneers who shaped our digital world.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: The best way to predict the future is to invent it.
The people who are crazy enough to think they can change the world are the ones who do.

collections.name: Literary Classics
collections.description: Memorable lines from the greatest works of literature throughout history.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: It was the best of times, it was the worst of times.
All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way.

collections.name: Shakespeare
collections.description: Timeless lines from the Bard's plays and sonnets, still echoing four centuries on.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: To be, or not to be, that is the question.
All the world's a stage, and all the men and women merely players.

collections.name: Oscar Wilde
collections.description: The dazzling wit and paradoxical epigrams of Oscar Wilde, drawn straight from his plays, novel, and essays.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: We are all in the gutter, but some of us are looking at the stars.
I can resist everything except temptation.

collections.name: Sherlock Holmes
collections.description: The sharp deductions, dry wit, and enduring friendship of Sherlock Holmes and Dr. Watson, drawn straight from Arthur Conan Doyle's canon.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: You see, but you do not observe. The distinction is clear.
Come, Watson, come! The game is afoot.

collections.name: Edgar Allan Poe
collections.description: Haunting lines from the gothic tales and shadow-haunted poems of Edgar Allan Poe, master of mystery and the macabre.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Quoth the Raven, "Nevermore."
All that we see or seem Is but a dream within a dream.

collections.name: Jane Austen
collections.description: Wit, romance, and razor-sharp social observation from the novels of England's most beloved chronicler of the human heart.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: You pierce my soul. I am half agony, half hope.
If I loved you less, I might be able to talk about it more.

collections.name: Great Poems
collections.description: The most unforgettable lines of verse ever written, from Frost's diverging roads to Dickinson's feathered hope.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes:
Two roads diverged in a wood, and I—
I took the one less traveled by,
And that has made all the difference.

I am the master of my fate,
I am the captain of my soul.

collections.name: Dystopian Fiction
collections.description: Chilling visions of the future from the great dystopian novels, where surveillance, conformity, and control reveal what we stand to lose.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Big Brother is watching you.
It was a pleasure to burn.

collections.name: Children's Literature
collections.description: Warm, wise lines from beloved children's books that comfort and delight readers of every age.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: A person's a person, no matter how small.
And the tree was happy.

collections.name: First Lines
collections.description: The unforgettable opening sentences that pull readers into the world's most beloved novels.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Call me Ishmael.
Mother died today.

collections.name: Mindfulness & Peace
collections.description: Calming quotes about presence, meditation, and inner peace.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: The present moment is filled with joy and happiness. If you are attentive, you will see it.
Peace comes from within. Do not seek it without.

collections.name: Gratitude & Thankfulness
collections.description: Quotes on appreciation, counting blessings, and the quiet power of a thankful heart.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Gratitude turns what we have into enough.
Gratitude makes sense of our past, brings peace for today, and creates a vision for tomorrow.

collections.name: Resilience & Grit
collections.description: Words for the hard days — on enduring adversity, bouncing back, and finding strength in the struggle.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: What does not kill me makes me stronger.
Fall seven times, stand up eight.

collections.name: Self-Compassion & Kindness
collections.description: Gentle reminders to treat yourself, and everyone you meet, with the kindness you deserve.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Talk to yourself like you would to someone you love.
Be kind whenever possible. It is always possible.

collections.name: Rest, Sleep & Balance
collections.description: Permission to slow down — quotes on the quiet art of rest, sleep, and a life in balance.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Early to bed and early to rise makes a man healthy, wealthy, and wise.
Sleep is the best meditation.

collections.name: Marvel Movies
collections.description: Iconic lines from Marvel superhero films spanning the MCU, X-Men, Spider-Man, and more.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: I am Iron Man.
With great power comes great responsibility.

collections.name: DC Comics & Films
collections.description: Legendary quotes from Batman, Superman, Wonder Woman, and the DC universe across comics and film.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Why so serious?
I'm Batman.

collections.name: LDS General Conference
collections.description: Faith-promoting words from prophets, apostles, and leaders of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, drawn from recent general conferences.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Because of Jesus Christ, our failures do not have to define us. They can refine us.
Worthiness is not flawlessness. Worthiness is being honest and trying.

collections.name: Disney Animated
collections.description: Magical quotes from Disney's animated classics, from the golden age to modern masterpieces.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Hakuna Matata! It means no worries for the rest of your days.
To infinity and beyond!

collections.name: Seinfeld
collections.description: Iconic quotes and catchphrases from the show about nothing that became everything.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: No soup for you!
Serenity now!

collections.name: How I Met Your Mother
collections.description: Legen— wait for it —dary lines and catchphrases from the gang at MacLaren's Pub.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Lawyered!
Have you met Ted?

collections.name: Fantasy Worlds
collections.description: Epic quotes from beloved fantasy books, films, and TV adaptations.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Not all those who wander are lost.
Winter is coming.

collections.name: Western Movies
collections.description: Legendary quotes from the Wild West, featuring outlaws, heroes, and frontier justice.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Go ahead, make my day.
I'm your huckleberry.

collections.name: Sci-Fi Screen
collections.description: Memorable quotes from science fiction movies and television that shaped our imagination.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: May the Force be with you.
Live long and prosper.

collections.name: Star Wars
collections.description: Iconic lines from a galaxy far, far away, spanning the Skywalker saga from the original trilogy to the prequels and sequels.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Do. Or do not. There is no try.
I find your lack of faith disturbing.

collections.name: Battlestar Galactica
collections.description: Lines of survival, faith, and frakkin' defiance from the reimagined Battlestar Galactica.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: So say we all.
All of this has happened before, and all of this will happen again.

collections.name: Bible Wisdom
collections.description: Timeless verses of faith, hope, and love from the pages of Scripture.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
Be still, and know that I am God.

collections.name: Words of Jesus
collections.description: The red-letter sayings of Jesus of Nazareth, from the Sermon on the Mount to the parables.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Blessed are the peacemakers: for they shall be called the children of God.
Judge not, that ye be not judged.

collections.name: Saints & Church Fathers
collections.description: Wisdom on faith, love, and the soul from the great saints and fathers of the Church.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Man proposes, but God disposes.
Ignorance of the Scriptures is ignorance of Christ.

collections.name: C. S. Lewis
collections.description: Reflections on faith, reason, and longing from the author of Mere Christianity and Narnia.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Courage, dear heart.
No one ever told me that grief felt so like fear.

collections.name: The Qur'an
collections.description: Verses of mercy, patience, and guidance from the Holy Qur'an.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: But lo! with hardship goeth ease,
Is the reward of goodness aught save goodness?

collections.name: Rumi
collections.description: Verses of love and the longing for the divine from the Sufi poet Jalal al-Din Rumi.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Listen to this reed how it complains, telling a tale of separations—
The lover's ailment is separate from all other ailments: love is the astrolabe of the mysteries of God.

collections.name: Prophetic Wisdom
collections.description: Sayings of the Prophet Muhammad on character, kindness, and faith.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: None of you truly believes until he loves for his brother what he loves for himself.
Do not think little of any good deed, even meeting your brother with a cheerful face.

collections.name: Bhagavad Gita
collections.description: Krishna's counsel to Arjuna on duty, devotion, and the eternal soul.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Life cannot slay. Life is not slain!
Let right deeds be thy motive, not the fruit which comes from them.

collections.name: The Buddha
collections.description: Teachings on the mind, suffering, and compassion from the Dhammapada.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: For hatred does not cease by hatred at any time: hatred ceases by love, this is an old rule.
If one man conquer in battle a thousand times thousand men, and if another conquer himself, he is the greatest of conquerors.

collections.name: Tao Te Ching
collections.description: Lao Tzu on the Way, simplicity, and the art of effortless action.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: The highest excellence is like (that of) water.
He who knows other men is discerning; he who knows himself is intelligent.

collections.name: Zen & the Dalai Lama
collections.description: Modern Buddhist wisdom on mindfulness, compassion, and peace.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: The purpose of our lives is to be happy.
No mud, no lotus.

collections.name: Jewish Wisdom
collections.description: Teachings on justice, learning, and repair of the world from Torah, Talmud, and the sages.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Who is rich? One who is happy with his portion.
Judge every person favorably.

collections.name: Sikh Wisdom
collections.description: Teachings of the Gurus on devotion, equality, and honest living from the Guru Granth Sahib.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Truth is higher than everything; but higher still is truthful living.
So why call her bad? From her, kings are born.

collections.name: Legendary Coaches
collections.description: Hard-won wisdom on winning, discipline, and teamwork from the greatest coaches in sports.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: We're on to Cincinnati.
Be quick, but don't hurry.

collections.name: Champion's Mindset
collections.description: Grit, drive, and greatness in the words of the athletes who reached the top.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Pressure is a privilege.
There you go. I'm the greatest.

collections.name: Yogi Berra
collections.description: The gloriously tangled wisdom of baseball's greatest philosopher, one Yogi-ism at a time.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Pair up in threes.
It's deja vu all over again.

collections.name: Entrepreneurs
collections.description: Building, betting, and beginning — hard lessons from founders who bet on themselves.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Screw it, let's do it.
Competition is for losers.

collections.name: On Leadership
collections.description: What it takes to lead — insight on trust, vision, and service from those who led.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Leadership belongs to those who take it.
Perpetual optimism is a force multiplier.

collections.name: Money & Investing
collections.description: Timeless sense about wealth, markets, and money from investors who built it.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Lost time is never found again.
Pain plus reflection equals progress.

collections.name: Great Speeches
collections.description: The lines that changed history, drawn from the world's most powerful addresses.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: I have nothing to offer but blood, toil, tears and sweat.
Free at last! Free at last! Thank God Almighty, we are free at last!

collections.name: Founding Fathers
collections.description: Liberty, virtue, and self-government in the words of America's founders.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Well done is better than well said.
My country is the world, and my religion is to do good.

collections.name: Civil Rights Voices
collections.description: Courage and conscience from the leaders who marched for freedom and equality.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Nobody's free until everybody's free.
I am sick and tired of being sick and tired.

collections.name: Cosmos & Space
collections.description: Wonder at the universe and the drive to explore it, from astronomers and astronauts.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Magnificent desolation.
I touch the future. I teach.

collections.name: Great Scientists
collections.description: Curiosity, doubt, and discovery in the words of history's great scientific minds.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: The secret is comprised in three words—Work, Finish, Publish.
I think I can safely say that nobody understands quantum mechanics.

collections.name: Curiosity & Discovery
collections.description: The restless spirit of invention and inquiry that pushes the boundary of the known.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Like what you do, and then you will do your best.
Machines take me by surprise with great frequency.

collections.name: Bob Dylan
collections.description: The Nobel laureate off the record — Dylan on art, fame, and freedom in his own words.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: I want to needle them.
I welcome them—with open arms.

collections.name: The Beatles
collections.description: Wit and wisdom from John, Paul, George, and Ringo — the Fab Four off the record.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: He must be blind.
I had one yesterday.

collections.name: Johnny Cash
collections.description: Faith, love, and hard-won redemption in the words of the Man in Black.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Any talent is a gift of God.
Give God's temple back to Him. The alternative is death.

collections.name: One-Liners
collections.description: Rapier wit in a single sentence, from the sharpest tongues in comedy and letters.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Take my wife... please.
What fresh hell can this be?

collections.name: Mark Twain's Wit
collections.description: The wisecracks and satire of America's greatest humorist, drawn from his own pen.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: When in doubt, tell the truth.
All right, then, I'll go to hell.

collections.name: Stand-Up Legends
collections.description: Deadpan, absurd, and observational — clean classics from the masters of stand-up.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Snub no man, nice cinnamon buns.
Procrastinate now, don't put it off.

collections.name: Love & Romance
collections.description: The timeless language of the heart, from the greatest poets and writers of love.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: How do I love thee? Let me count the ways.
You pierce my soul. I am half agony, half hope.

collections.name: Friendship
collections.description: On loyalty, kindred spirits, and the friends who shape a life.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: For his friend is another self.
A friend may well be reckoned the masterpiece of nature.

collections.name: Marriage & Weddings
collections.description: Words for the wedding toast and the long marriage after — on commitment and lasting love.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Whatever our souls are made of, his and mine are the same.
You must allow me to tell you how ardently I admire and love you.

collections.name: John Muir
collections.description: The rapture of the wild from America's great naturalist and champion of the mountains.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: The snow is melting into music.
In every walk with Nature one receives far more than he seeks.

collections.name: Thoreau & Walden
collections.description: Simplicity, solitude, and deliberate living from the sage of Walden Pond.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: We need the tonic of wildness.
Things do not change; we change.

collections.name: Earth & the Wild
collections.description: Reverence for the living world, from the great voices of nature and conservation.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: All flourishing is mutual.
In nature nothing exists alone.

collections.name: Studio Ghibli
collections.description: Wonder, courage, and quiet wisdom from the enchanted films of Studio Ghibli.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Ponyo wants ham!
May all your bacon burn!

collections.name: Naruto
collections.description: Ninja resolve, pain, and never giving up — iconic lines from the world of Naruto.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: The power of youth!
Sorry I'm late. I got lost on the path of life.

collections.name: One Piece
collections.description: Dreams, freedom, and the bonds of a crew — unforgettable lines from One Piece.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: I want to live!
Luffy… help me.

collections.name: Iconic Game Lines
collections.description: The unforgettable lines that gamers quote, from the classics to modern blockbusters.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Praise the Sun!
Ah, fresh meat!

collections.name: The Legend of Zelda
collections.description: Courage, wisdom, and power — memorable lines from across Hyrule's long history.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Open your eyes.
Thank you, Master Link.

collections.name: RPG Wisdom
collections.description: Philosophy from the pause menu — the lines that made role-playing games unforgettable.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Not until the end. Always.
Does this unit have a soul?

collections.name: StarCraft
collections.description: Battle cries and unit barks from the Koprulu Sector — the Terran, Protoss, and Zerg lines burned into every RTS player's memory.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: You must construct additional pylons.
Nuclear launch detected.

collections.name: Warcraft
collections.description: Zug zug — the peon barks, war cries, and villain lines that carried Warcraft from Azeroth's battlefields into gaming legend.
collections.author: Quips Editorial
previewQuotes: Work, work.
You are not prepared!
